channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: EHAB Launches Parametric Weather Platform for Brokers
item.description: EHAB (London), a climate-risk technology company specializing in weather intelligence, operational climate risk and parametric insurance infrastructure, has launched WeatherWise Studio, a parametric weather structuring and workflow platform for brokers, insurers and reinsurers. EHAB says the platform combines more than 45 years of global weather history at approximately 9 km resolution with a library of industry-specific parametric structures for sectors including renewable energy, construction, logistics, agriculture, hospitality, commercial real estate and infrastructure. The platform, which has been in beta with selected broker partners since January 2026, is designed to let brokers move from an initial client discussion to a structured parametric submission in under 30 minutes, according to the company.

item.title: NHTSA Opens Probe into 115,000 Rivian Vehicles
item.description: National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said on Thursday it has opened a preliminary probe into 114,922 Rivian vehicles over an issue with the rear toe link. NHTSA’s probe will assess the sensitivity of the rear toe link joint to foreseeable road and service conditions and evaluate Rivian’s current toe link repair procedure, among other steps. The agency’s office of defects investigation received two vehicle owner questionnaires reporting that the left rear toe link separates while driving, causing vehicles to swerve across multiple lanes of traffic, NHTSA said.

item.title: Distracted Driving Seen as Leading Cause of Work Zone Crashes in Carolinas: Survey
item.description: A new survey indicates that most work zone crashes in the Carolinas are the result of distracted driving, according to the Associated General Contractors of America (AGC). Ninety-five percent of South Carolina respondents and 93 percent of North Carolina respondents identified distracted drivers as a primary contributor to work zone crashes and intrusions. The survey results also reinforce the risks motorists face in work zone crashes.

item.title: Moody’s: U.S. Faces $375B in Uninsured Flood Losses From 1-in-100-Year Event
item.description: faces a widening gap between growing flood risk and the take-up of flood insurance, potentially exposing the nation to more than $375 billion in aggregated uninsured flood losses from a 1-in-100-year event, according to a new whitepaper from Moody’s. Counties in Florida, Louisiana, South Carolina and Texas face more than $5 billion in potential uninsured losses in the event of a 1-in-100-year flood. “Uninsured losses arise not from isolated outliers, but from persistent gaps between expanding flood hazards – particularly beyond regulatory flood maps that dictate mortgage requirements, as well as rarer, high-severity events – and insurance take-up,” Moody’s concluded.

item.title: Florida P/C Market Now Totals 20 Insurers
item.description: Three new property and casualty insurers have entered Florida’s market, according to an announcement last week by Insurance Commissioner Mike Yaworsky. Builder Reciprocal Insurance Exchange, Frontline Insurance Reciprocal Exchange, and Wingsail Insurance Company entered the market, marking 20 total insurers since Florida’s historic legislative reforms. The insurers bring in more than $850 million in new capital to the market, Yaworsy’s statement said.

item.title: Willis appoints Cheryl Lee as finex leader for Singapore
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Anthropic Nears $1T Valuation And Leapfrogs OpenAI On Unicorn Board With $65B Funding Round
item.description: Generative AI company Anthropic announced on Thursday that it has raised $65 billion in a Series H funding round, more than doubling its post-money valuation to a staggering $965 billion. In February, OpenAI announced it had closed a $110 billion round at an $840 billion post-money valuation. The financing also included $15 billion of previously committed investments from hyperscalers, including $5 billion from Amazon , which, interestingly, also participated in OpenAI’s most recent round of funding.

item.title: Oak Global secures 100% upsized $150m debut Quercian Re 2026-1 retro cat bond
item.description: Oak Global, the specialist Lloyd’s underwriting company, has successfully priced its debut catastrophe bond sponsorship, securing a 100% upsized $150 million of retrocessional protection from the capital markets from its Arthur Re Ltd. This now priced $150 million Quercian Re 2026-1 issuance will become the first catastrophe bond that will benefit Oak Reinsurance Syndicate 2843, a syndicate that’s focused on reinsurance opportunities that is operated by specialist underwriter in the Lloyd’s market Oak Global. – Quercian Re 2026-1 cat bond issuance, while the notes priced below initial guidance, Artemis has learned.

item.title: Federal insurance backstop to boost Canada’s resilience to earthquake risk: Morningstar DBRS
item.description: Morningstar DBRS, a credit rating agency focused on financial institutions and insurance, together with the Property and Casualty Insurance Compensation Corporation (PACICC), a Canadian industry-funded organisation that supports property and casualty policyholders, and the Insurance Bureau of Canada (IBC), the national industry association representing private property and casualty insurers, examine the ongoing policy discussion around introducing a federally supported insurance backstop for earthquake risk in Canada. The analysis also references the Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions (OSFI), Canada’s federal insurance regulator responsible for capital adequacy and prudential oversight, alongside international developments such as proposals from the European Insurance and Occupational Pensions Authority and the European Stability Mechanism aimed at strengthening catastrophe risk-sharing frameworks. Following this, PACICC and the Insurance Bureau of Canada have jointly proposed a structured backstop model inspired by the US Terrorism Risk Insurance Act (TRIA), under which the US government shares insured losses with private insurers once defined thresholds are reached.

item.title: Markets/Coverages: E&S Property MGA Luminor Risk Launches
item.description: Luminor Risk, a managing general agent specializing in the U.S. Atlanta-based Luminor said its platform pairs experienced underwriting leadership with modern data and modeling infrastructure, giving underwriters real-time analytical support across submission intake, risk selection, pricing, and portfolio construction. Topics Excess Surplus New Markets Insurance Wholesale Property

item.title: Cruise Operator Carnival Discloses Personal Data Breach
item.description: Cruise operator Carnival Corp said on May 27 it had detected a cybersecurity incident involving a compromised account of an employee in April, leading to the leak of certain personal information of individuals, including names, addresses and government-issued identification numbers. The company said it quickly blocked the unauthorized activity, which used social engineering to deceive an employee and gain access to the data. Carnival added it hired third-party security experts to conduct a thorough investigation.

item.title: Court Overturns Mass.-Based Insulet’s $59M Trade Secret Verdict
item.description: appeals court on Thursday overturned a $59 million verdict that medical device maker Insulet won against Korean rival EOFlowfor allegedly stealing trade secrets related to its insulin-pump technology. The Federal Circuit said the verdict could not stand based on the three-year statute of limitations for federal trade-secret claims. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit reversed the verdict after finding that Insulet had waited too long to bring its lawsuit.

item.title: Newrez expands HomeHub platform with Matic insurance comparison integration
item.description: Top-five US mortgage lender Newrez has expanded its partnership with insurtech Matic Insurance, bringing homeowners insurance comparison capabilities directly into its HomeHub customer portal as insurers and lenders increasingly focus on embedded digital experiences. “By fully integrating the Matic platform into the Newrez HomeHub experience, our homeowners will be able to explore real-time insurance savings, similar to how we proactively notify customers when they can save money on their mortgage,” said Leslie Gillin, Chief Commercial Officer at Newrez. The integration allows homeowners using HomeHub to compare insurance policies from more than 70 carriers, review their current coverage, and assess policy protection scores without leaving the platform.

item.title: The Wasted Insurance Opportunity in AI Subscriptions
item.description: Every few years, the insurance industry watches a new distribution channel open up and takes too long to walk through it. And almost no one in traditional insurance is talking about it. Anthropic confirmed that paid subscriptions to its Claude AI platform more than doubled in 2026.

item.title: Branch Insurance Exchange reports Q1 2026 results
item.description: Branch Insurance Exchange, the reciprocal managed by insurance startup Branch, has released its Q1 2026 results, ending the quarter with $20.3 million in written premiums, a 7% increase compared to the same period last year. Branch Insurance Exchange posted positive operating cash flow of $2.3 million in Q1 2026, its second consecutive positive quarter after a $1.8 million surplus in Q4 2025. Branch Financial has issued $173.5 million in surplus notes to the Exchange across 14 tranches between 2020 and 2024.

item.title: Corgi raises $106 million
item.description: After announcing $160 million in funding earlier this month, business insurance startup Corgi Insurance Services says it raised $106 million in a Series B1 round. This new injection of capital, which brings total funding to $378 million, values the company at $2.6 billion. The financing will support continued expansion of Corgi’s full-stack insurance platform and launch into new lines of commercial insurance.

item.title: CNN Files Suit Against Perplexity Alleging Unlawful Content Distribution
item.description: The CNN suit is the latest in a series of legal challenges brought against Perplexity, which uses AI to scour websites and answer users’ queries, alleging the company has infringed on copyrighted content and unlawfully scraped data to train its technology. CNN on Thursday filed a lawsuit against Perplexity, alleging the AI search engine provider is unlawfully distributing its copyrighted content, marking the latest legal tussle between the AI firm and a news publisher. “CNN’s lawsuit stands for the proposition that Perplexity, a company valued at tens of billions of dollars, should not be able to steal from entities that create the original content Perplexity exploits,” the Warner Bros.-owned news company said in a statement.

item.title: Opposition Could Delay Proposed $7B Settlement in Roundup Cancer Claims Lawsuits
item.description: Delays could be in store for a proposed $7.25 billion settlement covering thousands of claims that the maker of Roundup weedkiller failed to warn people the product could cause cancer. The dispute about who should preside over the proposed settlement could disrupt its deadlines and delay a resolution about whether it should be approved. Supreme Court weighs a case that could block thousands of lawsuits filed in state courts against agrochemical-maker Bayer, which added Roundup to its portfolio when it acquired Missouri-based Monsanto in 2018.

item.title: Tesla’s Former AI Trainers Don’t Trust its Self-driving Tech or Safety Stats
item.description: But interviews with nine former labelers and a former Tesla self-driving engineer show that the technology continued to struggle in recent months to execute basic maneuvers – such as avoiding emergency vehicles or stopping for school buses loading or unloading students. Inside Tesla, as these events approached, staffers worked long hours mapping routes and training the software on specific hazards to make the company’s self-driving technology appear more capable than it really is, four of the former Tesla employees told Reuters. In a Utah office, hundreds of Tesla workers scrutinize video collected by vehicles using the automaker’s Full Self-Driving (FSD) feature.

item.title: Firms have improved but must do more to prevent sanctions breaches
item.description: Financial firms have made progress in preventing sanctions breaches – with £37bn worth of assets frozen in the UK as of last year – but gaps remain, warns the FCA. Since February 2022, the FCA has proactively assessed the sanctions systems and controls of over 150 firms across a range of financial services sectors. In its latest review , the FCA found: The regulator is sharing good and poor practice it identified to help all firms further strengthen their sanctions controls.

item.title: Crunchbase Data: Venture Dollars For Black Startup Founders Stay Scarce Despite AI Funding Boom
item.description: venture funding going to companies with Black founders in 2025 remained low, even as overall startup investment ticked slightly higher, Crunchbase data shows. venture funding — went to startups with a Black founder or co-founder last year, per Crunchbase data. Last year’s total also represents a sharp decline from the record venture funding year of 2021, when investment in Black startup founders hit a high of $5.2 billion in the wake of the 2020 racial justice movement .

item.title: Bridging Africa’s Innovation Gap: From Potential To Power
item.description: In this new scenario, emerging innovation economies hold the potential for disrupting the agglomeration paradigm, toward a new scheme of interconnected networks of specialized local innovation hot spots. As of today, Africa’s innovation economy includes 883 tech scaleups that have raised a combined $24.7 billion. The African innovation landscape remains highly concentrated around four main hubs: South Africa, Egypt (North-East), Nigeria (West Africa) and Kenya (East Africa).

item.title: Plenum’s CAT Bond Dynamic Fund surges to $600m in AUM, delivers strong performance
item.description: Dirk Schmelzer, Head Portfolio Manager ILS/CAT Bonds and Partner at Plenum Investments AG, commented: “The growth of our fund can be attributed to several factors: the ongoing search for attractive yields, the fund’s very strong performance over the past five years, significantly increased geopolitical and trade policy risks, as well as the growing awareness of CAT bonds among a broader range of investors.” He continued: “At the same time, a new trend is emerging within the CAT bond fund market. Plenum Investments, the specialist manager of catastrophe bond and other reinsurance-linked assets, has announced that its Plenum CAT Bond Dynamic Fund has crossed approximately USD 600 million in assets under management, which according to the firm underscores the success of the fund’s efficiency-oriented investment approach. We reported back in December 2025, that the Plenum CAT Bond Dynamic Fund had surpassed $500 million.

item.title: Jamaica Govt praise investor support for new cat bond, plan parametric CCRIF cover expansion
item.description: Jamaica’s Prime Minister and Minster for Finance and Public Services both praised global investor support for the $200 million IBRD CAR Jamaica 2026 catastrophe bond, with the third cat bond a key pillar in the countries fiscal resilience and disaster risk financing, while also revealing they plan to expand parametric coverage under CCRIF. And I’m pleased to say that we floated now our third catastrophe bond successfully, which showed from the international community that there is an appetite for Jamaica and it was a great success that we were able to do that.” Holness also revealed plans to expand Jamaica’s parametric risk transfer through coverage under the CCRIF facility as well. Speaking yesterday the Jamaica leadership expressed thanks for the support from institutional and insurance-linked securities (ILS) investors for the countries largest catastrophe bond yet.

item.title: Florida cat bonds hit $3.2bn in 2026 YTD, pricing lower compared to last year: Guy Carpenter
item.description: Reinsurance buyers in Florida continue to turn to and leverage the catastrophe bond amidst rising appetite for risks from the state and softening of property catastrophe rates, with over $3.2 billion of new Florida-focused cat bonds having been issued thus far for 12 sponsors in 2026 year-to-date (up to May 12, 2026), according to Guy Carpenter. In its June 2026 Florida Reinsurance Renewal Report, broker Guy Carpenter highlights that in 2026 YTD, 42 different cat bonds were issued through 39 sponsors, with these transactions totalling over $10 billion in limit, which matches pace with the same point of activity from 2025. “Continued softening pricing in 2026 YTD and improved terms were key contributors to elevated activity, including from Florida-focused insurers,” Guy Carpenter added.

item.title: Convex receives in principle approval to launch long-tail specialty lines focused syndicate at Lloyd’s
item.description: Convex Group Limited, the international specialty insurer and reinsurer, has received in principle approval to launch Lloyd’s Syndicate 1987, which is planned to commence underwriting on 1st July 2026, subject to regulatory approvals. With capacity to support Convex’s approved business plan, Syndicate 1987 will focus exclusively on long-tail specialty lines, deploying capacity across a targeted portfolio of casualty re/insurance, as well as political risk and trade credit. This dual syndicate structure will provide a comprehensive footprint for International business, ensuring Convex is well positioned to meet the growing demands of its clients and brokers by delivering sustainable, accretive capacity across the entire spectrum of specialty lines.” Lorraine Harfitt, CEO at Asta, added, “We are delighted to manage Syndicate 1987 and support Convex with this new venture.

item.title: SCOR gets $75m of multi-year nat cat reinsurance with latest cat bond
item.description: SCOR, the France-based global reinsurance group, has completed the sponsorship of a new catastrophe bond, Atlas Capital DAC Series 2026-1, securing USD 75 million in multi-year protection against a range of natural catastrophe risks. Philipp Rüede, Group Chief Financial Officer of SCOR, added: “SCOR is pleased to sponsor a new cat bond this year, securing multi-year protection against peak natural perils from the ILS market at favourable pricing conditions. SCOR described Atlas Capital DAC Series 2026-1 as an aggregate, index-triggered catastrophe bond issued through Atlas Capital DAC, a multi-arrangement special purpose vehicle established in Ireland under Solvency II rules.

item.title: Global P&C insurance market stabilises as aggressive rate hikes fade: Allianz Research
item.description: The global property and casualty (P&C) insurance market is moving from a pricing boom towards normalisation after a period of aggressive rate hikes, according to Allianz Research’s latest “Global Insurance Report.” The insurer’s data shows that global P&C premiums grew by +3.8% in 2025, well below both the prior year’s +8.5% surge and the segment’s 10 year compound average growth rate (CAGR) of +5.6%. Supported by aging demographics and high savings, Asia remains the largest global life insurance market, analysts noted. While North America remained the industry’s dominant market, accounting for 52% of global P&C premiums, its growth plummeted to +2.2% from +9.7% in the previous year.

item.title: Merchant Ships Desert Strait of Hormuz Amid Renewed US Strikes
item.description: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz appeared all but deserted on Thursday as commercial operators remain wary of renewed military escalation .No commercial vessels were spotted transiting the waterway Thursday morning as tensions spiked following a second round of US strikes against Iranian military targets this week. Tehran also said 26 ships crossed the strait in the past day, which could include smaller, coastal vessels. President Donald Trump said no single nation would be allowed to control the Strait of Hormuz, underscoring one of the central obstacles to securing a lasting agreement with Iran.

item.title: CNN Files Lawsuit Against Perplexity Alleging Unlawful Content Distribution
item.description: CNN on Thursday filed a lawsuit against Perplexity in New York federal court , alleging the AI search engine provider is unlawfully distributing its copyrighted content, marking the latest legal tussle between the AI firm and a news publisher. “CNN’s lawsuit stands for the proposition that Perplexity, a company valued at tens of billions of dollars, should not be able to steal from entities that create the original content Perplexity exploits,” the Warner Bros.-owned WBD.O news company said in a statement. The CNN suit is the latest in a series of legal challenges brought against Perplexity, which uses AI to scour websites and answer users’ queries, alleging the company has infringed copyrights and unlawfully scraped data to train its technology.

item.title: WhatsApp, Meta Can Access Texans’ Private Messages, Lawsuit Claims
item.description: I am suing to protect Texans’ privacy and ensure that WhatsApp by Meta does not mislead Texans by unlawfully accessing private conversations and data.” For years, Meta has faced allegations and lawsuits that it can, in fact, view private messages sent on WhatsApp. Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton added WhatsApp to the growing list of companies he has accused of violating Texans’ data privacy, announcing a lawsuit against the messaging app and its parent company, Meta, last week. “WhatsApp cannot access people’s encrypted communications and any suggestion to the contrary is false,” Meta spokesperson Rachel Holland wrote in a statement responding to Paxton’s lawsuit.

item.title: Hippo introduces Hannah
item.description: Hippo announced it is scaling Hannah, its AI-powered service representative. So far in 2026, Hannah handled more than 28,000 service calls, replacing interactive voice response menus with a natural, conversational AI concierge that greets every Hippo customer, authenticates them, answers questions, and transfers them to a licensed agent whenever necessary. Most recently, Hannah began handling policy verification requests from mortgage lenders, which account for approximately 15% of Hippo’s inbound calls.

item.title: Sircon Compensation recognized for helping carriers turn agent compensation into a competitive advantage
item.description: The post Sircon Compensation recognized for helping carriers turn agent compensation into a competitive advantage appeared first on Coverager - Insurance news and insights . With solutions to manage agent onboarding, licensing, compensation and compliance across personal, commercial, life and health lines of business, Sircon for Carriers is powering Distribution Velocity for insurers so they can take advantage of every opportunity, adapt faster and grow smarter. Built specifically for insurance, Sircon Compensation helps carriers design and manage complex commission and incentive programs, automate payment processing, and gain real-time visibility into producer compensation across distribution channels.

item.title: Newrez partners with Matic
item.description: Top five mortgage lender Newrez is expanding its partnership with digital insurance marketplace Matic Insurance to bring homeowners insurance comparison into its HomeHub customer portal. “By fully integrating the Matic platform into the Newrez HomeHub experience, our homeowners will be able to explore real-time insurance savings, similar to how we proactively notify customers when they can save money on their mortgage. “We’re pleased to expand our partnership with Newrez and bring Matic’s network of over 70 carriers to Newrez’s digital ecosystem.

item.title: MSIG Europe impulsa su apuesta por el negocio marítimo y de transportes en España
item.description: MSIG Europe presentó ayer en Madrid su nueva línea de negocio especializada en transportes y marítimo , una iniciativa con la que «refuerza su posicionamiento en uno de los segmentos estratégicos para la compañía en Europa», afirman. José Luis Sánchez, country manager de MSIG Europe en España , destacó que este lanzamiento “fortalece la capacidad operativa y técnica de la compañía” y refuerza el compromiso de ofrecer soluciones “cada vez más completas y especializadas” para los clientes. Por su parte, Alex Mahnke, CMO de MSIG Europe, y Tom Oostra, CEO de MSIG Specialty Marine , subrayaron que esta nueva etapa permitirá acelerar el crecimiento de la compañía en segmentos estratégicos y responder con mayor agilidad a las necesidades de un mercado en constante transformación.

item.title: La venta necesita método: del seguimiento informal al control real de oportunidades
item.description: Pero la realidad es que cuesta responder a preguntas básicas: qué oportunidades están realmente vivas, cuáles necesitan seguimiento, qué presupuestos están pendientes de respuesta o por qué algunas operaciones se pierden. La actividad comercial en las corredurías es interminable, intensa, amplia, inacabable: llamadas, presupuestos enviados, contactos nuevos, clientes que preguntan, oportunidades que parecen avanzar y otras que se quedan paradas. Por eso, vender no puede consistir solo en generar oportunidades y confiar en que algunas acaben cerrándose.

item.title: Multirriesgos: ¿hacia dónde vamos?
item.description: En un contexto donde hogares, comercios y empresas son cada vez más tecnológicos y domotizados, la conectividad permitirá a las aseguradoras evolucionar hacia modelos de protección mucho más dinámicos y preventivos . Modelos de cobertura y tarificación que tengan en cuenta si un hogar, comercio o empresa dispone de sensores capaces de detectar una fuga antes de que provoque daños importantes, sistemas que identifiquen anomalías eléctricas, dispositivos conectados que monitoricen consumos o alerten de incidencias o avisos automáticos ante fenómenos meteorológicos extremos son solo algunos ejemplos de cómo el seguro podría evolucionar hacia una protección mucho más activa, inteligente y continua . La historia del seguro siempre ha ido ligada a la evolución de la sociedad.

item.title: Marsh: 9% Decrease in Transactional Risk Insurance Claims
item.description: Marsh Risk published its 2026 Global Transactional Risk Insurance Claims Report, providing a perspective on the transactional risk insurance claims landscape. Transactional risk claims with a major broker saw a 9% year-over-year decrease, a new report shows. Findings from the report are based on a review of Marsh’s 2025 global transactional risk claims data, focusing on metrics that include notification volumes, payment trends, prevalent breach types across notifications and payments and notification timing.

item.title: Zeroing in on Cognitive Imagery in Cotality Solutions
item.description: Today, we dive into the first of these pillars: cognitive imagery—the visual intelligence layer that Cotality is already using to transform underwriting and claims decision-making for carriers worldwide. Cotality’s cognitive imagery uses AI to amplify raw visuals with actionable truth. Cotality’s underwriting products leverage Cognitive Imagery to win this race to accuracy.

item.title: Amiga Specialty secures Lloyd’s capacity for new Southeast Asia-led yachts division
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Zurich New Zealand hires Alan James as head of distribution
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: AIG promotes veteran Tilden Goh to APAC head of energy
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Building Resilience in an Uncertain World
item.description: The theme of this year’s conference, “Building Resilience in an Uncertain World,” could not be more timely. These changes bring with them both challenges and opportunities, and they underscore the critical importance of resilience, particularly in the financial and insurance sectors. Today, I will share my perspective on the current geopolitical and economic outlook, the evolving risks we face, and the pivotal role the insurance sector can play in strengthening global resilience.

item.title: Sukate & Bezeboh Ltd enters administration
item.description: On 22 May 2026, Sukate & Bezeboh Ltd (SB Remit) entered administration. If you have any questions about the administration process, contact the joint administrators directly. Charles Turner and Frank Ofonagoro were appointed as joint administrators.

item.title: SCOR delighted with cat bond investor support, efficiency gains of reusable structure: Rüede
item.description: French global reinsurance company SCOR has highlighted its pleasure with the investor support received for its recently priced $75 million Atlas Capital DAC (Series 2026-1) catastrophe bond and the fact the issuance structure has now been reused for a fourth issuance, delivering efficiency gains. “SCOR is a longstanding sponsor of cat bonds, and we are delighted by the continued investor support, as cat bonds are an integral part of our risk partnerships strategy under the Forward 2026 plan.” Rüede also highlighted the fact the issuance vehicle has now been utilised for a fourth year in a row, saying, “We are also very pleased with the efficiency gains made by reusing Atlas Capital DAC for a fourth year.” SCOR has been sponsoring catastrophe bonds to access the capital for retrocession purposes since the year 2000 and you can read about all of them by filtering our Deal Directory by sponsor . SCOR also highlighted that, “The cat bond offering integrates ESG-related considerations to support investors’ due diligence.” The company said the cat bond was “well received” and “supported by healthy investor demand,” noting the assistance of GC Securities as Sole Structuring Agent and Sole Bookrunner for the deal, while Willkie Farr and Walkers advised SCOR as its legal counsels.

item.title: Florida renewal risk-adjusted pricing down 15% to 20% across many layers: Guy Carpenter
item.description: Property catastrophe reinsurance pricing has declined by 15% to 20% across many layers of the tower at the June 1st renewals according to broker Guy Carpenter, who highlighted improved property insurance market conditions, strong reinsurer balance-sheets and rising investor appetite as key drivers of the renewal dynamics. “Materially stronger balance sheets, improved underwriting performance, and sustained investor interest in Florida risk,” have all helped ceding companies derive better reinsurance renewal pricing at June 1st, Guy Carpenter explained. “Florida’s property market is on markedly stronger footing,” said Randy Fuller, Florida Segment Leader, Guy Carpenter.

item.title: Artemis London 2026 Super Early Bird rate ends soon. Register today to secure it
item.description: Having sold out of our initial allocation of Super Early Bird tickets faster than anticipated we added more, so there is still time to register for our next catastrophe bond and insurance-linked securities (ILS) market conference, Artemis London 2026 , at the best possible price. Register today to secure your place to attend Artemis London 2026 at the best possible price . We always try to keep conference prices reasonable for our community, so we increased the Super Early Bird ticket allocation after selling out quickly.

item.title: Amiga Specialty new Yacht division secures £/$25m Lloyd’s capacity and global licensing
item.description: Amiga Specialty, a specialist managing general agent (MGA), has secured £/$25 million of “A rated” Lloyd’s capacity with global licensing for its Yachts division, marking an important step in the development of its specialist marine offering. The newly secured capacity will bolster Amiga Specialty’s ability to underwrite complex yacht risks on a global scale. Thanks to the strong capabilities of Stephenson and the Amiga Yacht team, the division was able to gain access to highly-rated Lloyd’s capacity quickly and efficiently, according to the announcement.

item.title: VIG reports Q1’26 profit before taxes of €310.3m as GWP expand 4.4%
item.description: Vienna Insurance Group (VIG) has disclosed Q1 2026 profit before taxes of €310.3 million, a significant 18.8% increase over the same period of 2025, driven by the firm’s Special Markets, Poland, Extended CEE and Austria segments. “The very encouraging business development in the first quarter of 2026 means that we can confirm our outlook of achieving profit before taxes in the range of 1.25 to 1.30 billion euro (excluding Nürnberger) for 2026.” Meanwhile, VIG reported that gross written premiums expanded by 4.4% year on year to €4.857 billion in Q1 2026, with the strongest growth recorded in the Czech Republic (+8.4%), followed by Austria (+4.5%), and both Poland and the Extended CEE segment (+5.0% each).

item.title: Starr appoints Zhang as Global COO, Property
item.description: Starr, an insurance and investment organisation, has appointed Xinwei Zhang as its Global Chief Operating Officer (COO) for Property, tasked with leading the carrier’s globally aligned property operations function, strengthening team support, enhancing collaboration, and driving new growth opportunities. She has nearly two decades of experience leading property operations and driving transformative change at major global carriers. Most recently, she was the Chief Strategy Officer for Global Property at Allianz Commercial, where she led property transformation and strategies globally, including aligning MidCorp and LargeCorp Property processes, while supporting functions such as actuary, engineering, multinational, and operations.
